title: 11 Herbal Teas For Shingles
intro_desc: Herbal teas can be a great help when it comes to managing shingles. Shingles is a painful rash caused by the reactivation of the chickenpox virus, and it can be quite uncomfortable. Herbal teas, like those made from ginger (Zingiber officinale), can help to reduce pain and inflammation. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can ease the burning and itching sensations associated with shingles.  Another herb that can be beneficial is Echinacea purpurea, which is known for its immune-boosting properties. Drinking Echinacea tea can help your body fight off the virus that causes shingles, which can lead to a faster recovery. This can be especially helpful if you have a weakened immune system.  Lavandula angustifolia, also known as lavender, is another herb that can be used to help manage shingles symptoms. Lavender has a calming effect, which can help you relax and get a good night's sleep. Getting plenty of rest is essential when you have shingles, as it helps your body recover faster.  Drinking herbal teas can also help you feel better in other ways. For example, ginger tea can help ease nausea, which is a common symptom of shingles. Echinacea tea can help boost your mood and reduce stress, which can be a big help when you're not feeling well. And lavender tea can help you relax and get a good night's sleep, which is essential for recovery.  Herbal teas are also a natural and chemical-free way to manage shingles symptoms, which can be especially helpful if you're sensitive to medication. They're also easy to make at home, which can be a cost-effective option.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea, particularly those high in vitamin C and antioxidants, may help boost your immune system and prevent shingles. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. Some herbal teas, like echinacea and ginseng, have been studied for their potential antiviral properties. Their impact on shingles is still being explored.
faq_prevent: Some people think that drinking certain herbal teas, like echinacea or peppermint, might help prevent shingles. These teas are thought to have anti-inflammatory properties that could help boost the immune system and reduce the risk of shingles outbreaks. However, there's limited research on this topic, and more studies are needed to confirm any potential benefits.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas daily for shingles might not be suitable for everyone. Some teas can interact with medications or worsen symptoms. For instance, valerian root can make shingles symptoms worse in some people. It's essential to be cautious and observe how your body reacts to different herbs.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with shingles symptoms, but it's hard to say exactly when you'll feel better. Some people report relief within a few days, while others may need a week or more. It's also possible that you might not notice a difference at all. Everyone's body is different, so it's a case-by-case thing.
faq_time: For shingles, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This helps you feel more alert and focused, which can be helpful when dealing with symptoms like pain and fatigue. The tea's calming effects can also provide a gentle boost throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like St. John's Wort, can affect how prescription medications work. This includes medications for shingles, like antivirals and pain relievers. If you're taking prescription medications for shingles and also drinking herbal teas, the teas might interfere with the medications' effectiveness or increase the risk of side effects.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant and experiencing shingles symptoms, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can affect the baby's development or interact with other medications. For example, peppermint and ginger teas can help soothe shingles pain, but others like echinacea and licorice root may have unknown effects on the fetus.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for shingles may be tempting, but their safety during breastfeeding isn't well understood. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can pass into breast milk and affect the baby. If you're breastfeeding and have shingles, it's best to stick to proven treatments and avoid herbal remedies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce pain and inflammation in the body, which is beneficial for people suffering from shingles. Gingerol and shogaol also have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help alleviate nerve pain and muscle spasms associated with shingles. The anti-inflammatory properties of these constituents can also help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. By drinking Zingiber officinale teas, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms due to its ability to reduce pain, inflammation, and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried ginger root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Add the ginger root to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the ginger root.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains the bioactive constituents alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to stimulate the immune system, which is essential for fighting off the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. Glycosides, on the other hand, have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin and reduce the risk of scarring. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals may experience relief from shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea root and flowers.
Combine the root and flowers in a small bowl.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the mixture in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain into another cup.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, as needed for shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas contains a compound called linalool, which is a key active constituent that helps with shingles. Linalool has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with shingles. Lavandula angustifolia tea also contains linalyl acetate, another active constituent that has a soothing effect on the skin and can help to reduce pain and discomfort. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can also help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Lavandula angustifolia tea may provide relief from the symptoms of shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried flowers to a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help manage shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains boiactive constituents like luteolin, apigenin, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Melissa officinalis teas also contains antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress and promote healing in the affected area. The antiviral properties of the tea's constituents, such as luteolin, may help reduce the severity and duration of shingles. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Melissa officinalis teas can provide relief from shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, after consulting with a doctor or healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas contains compounds like quinine and alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help reduce the pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Quinine, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, the virus responsible for shingles. The alkaloids in Cinchona officinalis teas, including quinovic acid and cinchonine, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate the skin rash and lesions associated with shingles. Drinking Cinchona officinalis teas may help alleviate the symptoms of shingles, such as pain and discomfort, by targeting the underlying cause of the condition.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before meals.
Consult a doctor before using Cinchona officinalis tea for shingles, especially if you have any medical conditions or allergies.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Glycyrrhizin is a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort associated with shingles. Glycyrrhizin also has antiviral properties that can help to combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin can also help to reduce swelling and redness in the affected area, making it a potential treatment for shingles.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. If using fresh, dry them in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2-3 hours.
Grind 1-2 tablespoons of the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan over medium heat.
Add 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of the ground roots to the hot water and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, for 7-10 days. Monitor your symptoms and adjust the dosage as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are active constituents that help with shingles. Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain associated with shingles. Terpenoids, including ginkgolide A and B, have antiviral properties that target the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles. These compounds also improve blood flow to the affected area, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications. By addressing inflammation, virus replication, and promoting recovery, Ginkgo biloba teas may provide relief from shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Boil the water in a pot and let it cool for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day, as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains a compound called cineole, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. Cineole has been shown to help reduce pain and inflammation, making it a potential aid in managing shingles symptoms. Eucalyptus globulus teas also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can help combat oxidative stress. The antimicrobial properties of eucalyptus globulus can help fight off the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. By consuming eucalyptus globulus teas, some people experience relief from shingles symptoms, such as pain, itching, and skin lesions.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried leaves or 2 tablespoons of fresh leaves.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, as needed, to help relieve shingles symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas contains essential oils like carnosic acid, rosmarinic acid, and camphor. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles. The rosmarinic acid in Rosmarinus officinalis teas has been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes zoster virus, which causes shingles. Carnosic acid also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of shingles symptoms. The antioxidant properties of Rosmarinus officinalis teas may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of complications.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of honey or sweetener (optional).
Combine the boiling water and dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the leaves.
Add the honey or sweetener to taste, if using. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas contains the bioactive constituents hyperforin and emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, making them beneficial for people suffering from shingles. Hypericum perforatum teas also has antiviral properties, which can help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. Additionally, the tea's antioxidant properties can help to promote healing and reduce the severity of shingles symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of hyperforin and emodin may also help to reduce the duration and severity of shingles outbreaks.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Get 2 tablespoons of dried Hypericum perforatum leaves. You can buy it from a health food store or online.
Use 1 cup of boiling water to make the tea. Pour the boiling water over the dried leaves in a cup.
Let the leaves steep in the water for 5-7 minutes. This will help the active ingredients release into the water.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup. Discard the leaves.
Drink 1-2 cups of the Hypericum perforatum tea, 3-4 times a day. You can add honey or lemon to taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa teas contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles. Curcumin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and LOX, which contribute to the severity of shingles symptoms. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of curcumin can help protect against nerve damage and promote healing in the affected areas. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa teas may offer a natural and effective way to manage shingles symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for shingles
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried turmeric root, 1 cup of boiling water, a tea infuser or a small strainer, and a cup.
Add the turmeric root to the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the turmeric root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into the cup and discard the turmeric root.
Drink 1-2 cups of the turmeric tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
